Title: Payment Capture — Manual Capture
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Order exists with payment in Authorized status via manual payment method
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/orders
2. Open an order with payment in Authorized status
3. Open the payment document from the Documents section
4. Verify the Capture button is visible and enabled
5. Click the Capture button
6. Confirm the capture action in the dialog
7. Verify the payment status transitions
Expected Result:
- Capture button is visible when payment is in Authorized status
- Capture confirmation dialog is displayed
- Payment status changes from Authorized to Paid after capture
- Payment document displays updated status immediately
- Audit log records the capture action with timestamp

Title: Payment Refund — Full Refund
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Order exists with payment in Paid status
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/orders
2. Open an order with payment in Paid status
3. Open the payment document from the Documents section
4. Verify the Refund button is visible and enabled
5. Click the Refund button
6. Enter the full payment amount as the refund amount
7. Confirm the refund action
8. Verify the payment status transitions
Expected Result:
- Refund button is visible when payment is in Paid status
- Refund dialog allows entering amount up to the full payment value
- Payment status transitions from Paid to Refunded after full refund
- Refund document is created and linked to the payment
- Order totals reflect the refund correctly

Title: Payment Refund — Partial Refund
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Order exists with payment in Paid status with amount > $10
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/orders
2. Open an order with payment in Paid status
3. Open the payment document from the Documents section
4. Click the Refund button
5. Enter a partial amount (less than the total payment amount)

Expected Result:
- Partial refund is accepted with amount less than total
- Payment status remains Paid (not fully refunded)
- Refund document is created with the partial amount
- Refund amount is displayed correctly in the refund document
- Subsequent refund is possible for remaining balance

Title: Payment Status Transitions — Authorized to Captured to Refunded
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Order exists with manual payment method
Payment is in New status
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/orders and open the target order
2. Open the payment document
3. Transition payment from New to Authorized (click Authorize)
4. Verify payment is in Authorized status
5. Click Capture to transition to Paid/Captured
6. Verify payment is in Paid status
7. Click Refund and enter full amount to transition to Refunded
8. Verify final Refunded status
9. Verify each status transition is logged in the change history
Expected Result:
- Payment transitions correctly through each stage: New to Authorized to Paid to Refunded
- Each transition button appears only when the payment is in the correct prerequisite status
- Status badges update immediately after each transition
- Change log records each transition with timestamp and acting user
- No backward transitions are available once in terminal states

Title: Order Changes Widget — Change Log Tracks All Modifications
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Order exists with at least one prior modification
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/orders and open an existing order
2. Make a modification to the order (e.g., update customer comment)
3. Save the changes
4. Open the Changes widget on the order details blade
5. Verify the latest modification appears in the change log
6. Make a second modification (e.g., update dynamic property)
7. Save and re-open Changes widget
8. Verify both changes are tracked with timestamps and user info
Expected Result:
- Changes widget displays a chronological log of all order modifications
- Each log entry shows: field changed, old value, new value, timestamp, and user who made the change
- Most recent changes appear at the top of the log
- Changes widget refreshes after saving new modifications without requiring page reload
- System-generated changes (status transitions) are also captured

Title: Order Validation Full Field List — Complete Validation Test
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (API/Swagger)
Steps:
1. Open Swagger UI at ${BACK_URL}/docs or use API client
2. Test order validation for fields: Number, CustomerId, CustomerName, StoreId, Currency, Status, CreatedDate
3. Submit invalid value for Number field (e.g., empty string or exceeding max length)
4. Submit invalid value for CustomerId (e.g., null or non-existent ID)
5. Submit invalid value for CustomerName (e.g., exceeding character limit)
6. Submit invalid value for StoreId (e.g., non-existent store)
7. Submit invalid Currency code (e.g., invalid ISO code)
8. Submit invalid Status value (e.g., non-existent status string)
9. Submit invalid CreatedDate (e.g., future date or wrong format)
Expected Result:
- Number field returns validation error for empty or oversized values
- CustomerId returns error for null or non-existent customer
- CustomerName returns error when exceeding maximum length
- StoreId returns validation error for non-existent store reference
- Currency returns error for invalid ISO 4217 currency code
- Status returns error for values outside allowed status enumeration
- CreatedDate returns error for invalid date format or out-of-range values
- Each validation error message clearly identifies the offending field
